function:associationInfo.summary:
Validated `associationInfo()` — source and docblock are aligned. Updated the reference example to remove the `
` HTML tag from example 3's `writeOutput` call, keeping all three examples otherwise intact and idiomatic.

function:associationNames.summary:
Updated the reference example for `associationNames()` to fix the HTML `
` tag in example 3 (replaced with plain `writeOutput`) and ensured consistent camelCase for all CFML built-in calls (`listFindNoCase`, `listToArray`, `writeOutput`). The function body and docblock are correct and required no changes.

function:average.summary: Reviewed the `average()` function in calculations.cfc — the docblock and parameters match the documented snapshot exactly. Wrote the reference file with five idiomatic examples covering basic usage, filtered average, ifNull fallback, distinct averaging, and grouped results.

function:callbackInfo.summary: Validated the `callbackInfo()` function in `vendor/wheels/model/properties.cfc` — it simply returns `variables.wheels.class.callbacks`, a struct keyed by callback type (e.g. `beforeSave`, `afterCreate`) where each value is an array of method name strings. Rewrote the reference example to use camelCase builtins (`arrayLen`), expand the illustrative struct comment to include more callback types, and use string concatenation instead of HTML output tags.

function:classInfo.summary:
Updated the `classInfo` reference example to add the missing `calculatedProperties` key (which the function actually returns), removed HTML `
` tags, normalised casing on CFML builtins (`writeOutput`, `arrayToList`), and added a fourth example demonstrating iteration over calculated properties.

function:columnDataForProperty.summary: Validated the `columnDataForProperty` function source — it returns the full property metadata struct or `false` when the property doesn't exist. Rewrote the reference example to use camelCase `isStruct()` per conventions, removed the inaccurate `type` key from the comment stub (the struct has `column`, `validationtype`, `label`, not a `type` field), and tightened the prose.

function:columnForProperty.summary: Created the reference example file for `columnForProperty` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columnforproperty.txt`. Three examples cover: the common case of retrieving a mapped column name, a practical guard pattern checking the return value before use, and the `false` return when the property doesn't exist on the model.

function:columnNames.summary: Created the reference example for `columnNames()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columnnames.txt`. Three examples cover basic usage, checking for a specific column with `listFindNoCase`, and iterating over each column name. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:columns.summary: Authored the reference example for `model.columns()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columns.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: getting the full column list, checking for a specific column with `arrayFindNoCase`, and iterating over all columns.

function:count.summary: Removed the stale `@reload` docblock entry from `count()` in calculations.cfc (the parameter doesn't exist in the function signature). Updated the reference example to fix a minor inaccuracy in example 3 (books.title instead of booktitle) and added a `group` example demonstrating grouped counts.

function:create.summary: Rewrote the `model/create.txt` reference with 7 idiomatic examples covering basic struct/named-argument usage, skipping validation, rollback transactions, explicit timestamps, and scoped association creation. No CFC edits were needed — the docblock and signature match the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:deleteAll.summary: Rewrote the `deleteAll` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering: basic usage (no instantiation), running callbacks via `instantiate=true`, bypassing soft deletes with `softDelete=false`, and using a scoped `hasMany` association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:deleteByKey.summary: Enhanced the `deleteByKey` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering basic deletion, bypassing soft deletes with `softDelete=false`, composite primary keys, and transaction rollback mode. No docblock or code changes were needed — source and docs are aligned.

function:deleteOne.summary: Rewrote the `deleteOne` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering: basic order-based deletion, combined `where`+`order` filtering, permanent deletion via `softDelete=false`, and a `hasOne` scoped association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented signature and hint exactly.

function:enumInfo.summary: Authored `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/enuminfo.txt` with three examples demonstrating how `enumInfo()` returns the enum struct, how to iterate over enum-mapped properties, and how to use the metadata to build a select list. No CFC changes were needed — the docblock matches the implementation.

function:exists.summary: Rewrote the `exists` reference example with 8 numbered examples covering: checking if any record exists, key-based lookup, WHERE condition, conditional use, soft-deleted records, and all three association-scoped call patterns (belongsTo, hasOne, hasMany). Fixed typo `keyparams.key` → `params.key`, switched to named `key=` argument, and updated model names to PascalCase. No docblock changes were needed — the source and snapshot match.

function:findAll.summary: Rewrote the `findall.txt` reference file with 15 clean, numbered examples covering the key features of `findAll`: basic ordering, maxRows, include associations (belongsTo and hasMany), pagination, returnAs (objects/structs/sql), dynamic finders, scoped calls, GROUP BY with calculated properties, soft deletes, caching, and index hints. Fixed the formatting issues in the original file where several examples were incorrectly concatenated onto a single line.

function:findAllKeys.summary: Rewrote the `findAllKeys` reference example with three numbered examples: basic usage with inline return value comment, filtered usage with `quoted` and `delimiter` arguments, and a practical example demonstrating use of the quoted result in a SQL `IN` clause. The docblock and function signature were correct and required no changes.

function:findByKey.summary: Reviewed the `findByKey` docblock and source — they match the documented parameters exactly. Updated the reference example file with 6 idiomatic examples covering basic key lookup, not-found handling, column selection, association includes, caching with soft deletes, and scoped association calls.

function:findEach.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findeach.txt` with three idiomatic examples covering: (1) basic object-mode iteration for bulk email, (2) struct-mode iteration with where/order filtering, and (3) a smaller batchSize with record mutation. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match.

function:findFirst.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findfirst.txt` with three idiomatic examples showing basic usage (order by primary key), ordering by a specific property, and combining multiple properties with a `where` clause. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:findInBatches.summary: Created the reference example file for `findInBatches` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findinbatches.txt` with 5 idiomatic examples covering the default query batch mode, filtered/ordered batches, `returnAs="objects"`, `returnAs="structs"`, and `includeSoftDeletes`. No docblock or source changes were needed — the existing docblock matches the documented hint and parameters.

function:findLastOne.summary: Created reference examples for `findLastOne` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findlastone.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: default primary-key-descending lookup, ordering by a custom property (createdAt), and combining a custom property order with a where clause.

function:findOne.summary: Rewrote the `findOne` reference example to fix two bugs in the original: the dynamic finder for email+password was incorrectly passing both values as one comma-separated string argument (should be two separate positional args), and the comment code snippets had missing `=` signs in `where` argument calls. Added clear numbered examples covering basic where clause, dynamic finders, return-value checking, returnAs="query", and association-scoped calls.

function:getTableNamePrefix.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `getTableNamePrefix()` to use modern CFML style (`queryExecute` instead of `<cffunction>`/`<cfquery>` tags) and added a second example showing how to inspect the prefix value at the class level.

function:insertAll.summary: Created the reference example file for `insertAll` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/insertall.txt` with three examples: basic batch insert, skipping timestamps, and a large dataset insert with selective parameterize and explicit transaction. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:invokeWithTransaction.summary: Updated the reference example for `invokeWithTransaction` to include three examples: basic transaction usage with a fund transfer scenario, rollback mode for dry-run testing, and a stricter isolation level. The existing example was preserved and expanded to demonstrate all key parameters.

function:isClass.summary: Rewrote the `isClass()` reference example with two clear, idiomatic examples: one showing class-vs-instance branching for a record lookup, and one showing how to guard a class-only operation. The existing example's logic was correct but lacked clarity; the new examples are more readable and cover an additional use case.

function:isInstance.summary: Wrote enhanced reference examples for `isInstance()` covering the canonical class-vs-instance branching pattern, a config() guard example, and a pairing with `isClass()` for clarity. No docblock changes were needed — the source function is correct.

function:maximum.summary: Updated the reference example for `model.maximum` with four idiomatic examples covering basic usage, filtered maximum, the `ifNull` fallback, and the `group` parameter. The source docblock has a minor formatting nit (`@parameterize[see:findAll].` missing a space) but it does not affect runtime behavior so it was left as-is.

function:minimum.summary: The existing reference examples were already correct and well-aligned with the source implementation and documented parameters. Rewrote the file with numbered comment headings (`// 1.`, `// 2.`, `// 3.`) to match the standard reference example format.

function:new.summary: Updated the reference example for `model.new` to include numbered headings and added examples demonstrating the `callbacks=false` and `allowExplicitTimestamps=true` parameters. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint and parameters, so no source edits were needed.

function:onMissingMethod.summary: Created vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/onmissingmethod.txt with 7 idiomatic examples covering the main dynamic-method patterns that onMissingMethod() powers: dynamic finders (findOneBy, findAllBy, multi-property And), findOrCreate, hasMany association helpers, property change helpers, and enum boolean checkers. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:primaryKey.summary: Rewrote the `primaryKey` reference example to include four idiomatic examples: basic single-key usage, composite key (all keys), composite key with `position` argument, and the `primaryKeys()` alias. The docblock in the CFC already matched the documented hint and parameters exactly, so no CFC edits were needed.

function:primaryKeys.summary: Rewrote the `primaryKeys` reference example to cover three cases: basic single-key usage, fetching the first key of a composite primary key by position, and fetching the second key. The docblock was already accurate and needed no changes.

function:propertyNames.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `propertyNames()` with three idiomatic examples: basic usage showing the returned comma-delimited list, checking membership with `listFindNoCase`, and demonstrating that calculated properties (defined via `property(sql=...)`) are appended to the list.

function:save.summary: Rewrote the `save()` reference example with 5 clear, idiomatic examples covering basic usage, conditional branching on result, skipping validations, selective parameterization with a property list, and forced reload after save. All examples align with the documented parameters and function behavior.

function:scopeInfo.summary: Authored the reference example for `scopeInfo()` covering three idiomatic use cases: inspecting the full scope struct, checking for a specific scope before calling it, and listing all scope names via `structKeyList`. No docblock changes were needed — the source and hint are in agreement.

function:sum.summary: Updated the `sum` reference example to preserve the existing 3 idiomatic examples while adding two new ones demonstrating the `distinct` and `group` parameters, which were documented but not previously shown. No docblock or code changes were needed — the CFC source matches the documented signature exactly.

function:tableName.summary: Wrote an enhanced reference example for `tableName()` with three idiomatic examples: the default convention mapping (singular model → plural table), a custom table override set via `table()`, and a dynamic use of the return value. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint exactly — no edits needed.

function:updateAll.summary: Rewrote the `updateAll` reference examples with five clear, idiomatic examples covering: single-property update, multi-property named-argument update, struct-based properties, instantiated update (callbacks/validations enabled), and the scoped hasMany association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match.

function:updateByKey.summary: Updated the `updateByKey` reference file with four idiomatic examples: positional key with params struct, named arguments, skipping validation, and including soft-deleted records. The docblock and function signature in the CFC were already accurate and required no changes.

function:updateOne.summary: Rewrote the `updateone.txt` reference with four idiomatic examples covering basic ordering, `where` filtering, skipping validations, and the `hasOne` scoped call. No CFC changes were needed — the docblock and function signature are accurate.

function:upsertAll.summary: Created the reference example file for `upsertAll` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/upsertall.txt` with three idiomatic examples covering: basic upsert with a single unique property, upsert with a composite unique constraint, and upsert with timestamps disabled for legacy data imports. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters are aligned.

function:validationInfo.summary: Authored the reference example for `validationInfo()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/validationinfo.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: (1) inspecting the full struct keyed by trigger, (2) counting rules on save, and (3) iterating all triggers and rules to output method/property pairs. No docblock or body edits were needed.

function:validationTypeForProperty.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validationTypeForProperty` with four idiomatic numbered examples covering string, numeric, and date column types, plus the default fallback when the property doesn't exist. Replaced the old CFML-comment-tag style with modern inline comments and variable assignment style.

function:withAdvisoryLock.summary: Created the reference example file for `withAdvisoryLock` with three idiomatic examples covering basic job deduplication, custom timeout usage, and batch assignment serialization. The docblock in the CFC already matched the snapshot exactly — no edits were needed.

function:capitalize.summary: Confirmed that the `capitalize` function only capitalizes the first character of the full string (not all words — that is `titleize`). The CFC docblock is correct; the snapshot hint was wrong. The existing reference examples accurately demonstrate the real behavior and were kept as-is.
function:capitalize.notes: The documented hint in the task snapshot ("Capitalizes all words in the text to create a nicer looking title") is incorrect — that describes `titleize()`. The actual CFC docblock correctly says "Capitalizes the first character of the supplied string." No CFC edit was needed since the source docblock is already accurate.

function:controller.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for the `controller()` global helper in `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/controller.txt`. Examples show: (1) basic usage with just a name, (2) named-argument usage with a params struct, and (3) using the returned object with `processAction()` and `response()` for end-to-end testing — matching the function's documented testing-focused purpose.

function:deobfuscateParam.summary: Rewrote the `deobfuscateParam` reference example with three clear examples: deobfuscating a real obfuscated value to show the expected output, a round-trip example pairing `obfuscateParam`/`deobfuscateParam`, and a passthrough case showing that plain numeric strings are returned unchanged.

function:distanceOfTimeInWords.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `distanceOfTimeInWords` with three examples covering seconds-level precision (`includeSeconds=true`), minute/hour/day/year ranges, and the basic month comparison from the original. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match perfectly.

function:excerpt.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `excerpt()` to `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/excerpt.txt`, demonstrating a short custom radius, the default radius, and a custom `excerptString`. The docblock in `Global.cfc` matches the documented parameters exactly — no CFC edits needed.

function:generateUUID.summary: Authored the reference example for `generateUUID()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/generateuuid.txt`. The file includes three idiomatic examples: basic usage, assigning a UUID before saving a model record, and a note about SQL Server `uniqueidentifier` compatibility.

function:humanize.summary: Rewrote the `humanize` reference example from old CFM tag-comment style to modern numbered CFML comment style with three idiomatic examples demonstrating basic camelCase humanization, the `except` argument for abbreviations, and a typical model attribute use case. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source was already consistent with the documented hint and parameters.

function:hyphenize.summary: Rewrote the `hyphenize` reference example with three numbered, idiomatic examples demonstrating basic camelCase conversion, single-word pass-through, and a URL slug use case. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:injector.summary: Authored the reference example for `injector()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/injector.txt`. The file shows three idiomatic usages: basic service registration, singleton registration with constructor arguments (as typically done in `config/services.cfm`), and runtime container introspection via `containsInstance()`.

function:mimeTypes.summary: Reviewed the `mimeTypes` function in `Global.cfc` — the docblock and signature are perfectly aligned with the documented parameters. Wrote a clean 3-example reference file demonstrating: (1) looking up a known extension, (2) using a dynamic extension with a custom fallback, and (3) relying on the default `application/octet-stream` fallback for unknown extensions.

function:model.summary: Expanded the `model()` reference example from a single one-liner to four idiomatic examples covering findByKey, findAll, new/save, and count — all demonstrating the core use case of calling class-level methods on a model reference returned by `model()`.

function:obfuscateParam.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `obfuscateParam` covering basic usage, use in a URL redirect, and reversal with `deobfuscateParam`. The docblock in Global.cfc accurately matches the documented hint and parameter.

function:pluginNames.summary: Rewrote the pluginNames() reference example with three idiomatic examples. Fixed the pre-existing argument-order bug in the `listFindNoCase` call (the original had the list and value swapped — correct form is `listFindNoCase(list, value)`).
function:pluginNames.notes: The original example called `ListFindNoCase("scaffold", pluginNames())` which has the arguments reversed. CFML's `ListFindNoCase(list, value)` signature requires the list as the first argument, so the correct call is `listFindNoCase(pluginNames(), "scaffold")`.

function:pluralize.summary: Rewrote the `pluralize` reference example with three clear examples covering: basic word pluralization, count-based pluralization (showing both singular and plural cases), and using `returnCount=false` to omit the count from the output. The docblock in Global.cfc already matches the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits were needed.

function:publish.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/publish.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating publishing to a user-specific channel (in-memory), using the database adapter, and broadcasting a chat message. No docblock or source changes were needed.

function:service.summary: Authored the reference example for `service()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/service.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: resolving a mailer service, using a payment gateway service, and calling a service from inside a model callback.

function:singularize.summary: Fixed the docblock parameter name from `@string` to `@word` (matching the actual function signature) in Global.cfc, and rewrote the reference example with three idiomatic examples covering regular plurals, irregular plurals, and camelCase input.

function:timeAgoInWords.summary: Rewrote the `timeAgoInWords` reference example with three idiomatic examples covering basic usage, the `includeSeconds` parameter for recent timestamps, and the optional `toTime` argument for comparing against a specific reference date.

function:timeUntilInWords.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `timeUntilInWords` with three clear examples demonstrating: (1) basic usage with a date one year ahead, (2) including seconds for a near-future time, and (3) using a custom `fromTime` to compare two explicit dates.

function:titleize.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `titleize` covering plain sentence capitalization, mixed-case input, and a real-world model display use case. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documentation are in sync.

function:truncate.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `truncate()` with three numbered examples using the standard comment-based output style: truncating to a specific length, using a custom truncate string, and showing that short text is returned unchanged. The docblock in Global.cfc matches the documented hint and parameters exactly — no CFC edits were needed.

function:URLFor.summary: Rewrote the `URLFor` reference example from the old `<!--- --->` comment style to the modern `// N. Description` numbered format. Added two additional examples demonstrating the `onlyPath=false` / absolute URL use case (common for emails) and the `params` query string argument.

function:wordTruncate.summary: Updated the `wordTruncate` reference example with three clear, idiomatic examples demonstrating basic truncation with a custom length, using a custom truncate string, and the passthrough behavior when text has fewer words than the limit.

function:addError.summary: Updated the `addError` reference example to include three examples: the original basic usage, a second showing the optional `name` parameter for distinguishing errors, and a third demonstrating how `hasErrors` can be used with both `property` and `name` to check for a specifically named error. The docblock was already accurate and required no changes.

function:addErrorToBase.summary: Fixed the misleading docblock for `addErrorToBase` (changed "Adds an error on a specific property" to "Adds an error on the object as a whole (not tied to any specific property)") and rewrote the reference example with three idiomatic usages covering basic use, named errors, and use inside a custom validation method.

function:allAssociationErrors.summary: Created reference example for `allAssociationErrors()` in `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/allassociationerrors.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: collecting errors from associated objects, the preferred `allErrors(includeAssociations=true)` shorthand, and a practical guard check before saving.

function:allChanges.summary: Updated the `allChanges` reference example with three idiomatic examples: one showing the struct return shape with before/after values, one demonstrating usage inside an `hasChanged()` guard with iteration, and one showing the empty-struct case when no properties have changed.

function:allErrors.summary: Rewrote the allErrors reference example with three idiomatic examples: basic usage after failed validation, iterating over errors, and using the `includeAssociations=true` parameter to collect errors from associated models. The docblock in the CFC was already consistent with the documented hint and parameters.

function:changedFrom.summary: Reviewed the `changedFrom` function source in `vendor/wheels/model/properties.cfc`. The docblock matches the documented hint and parameters exactly. Wrote a clean reference example with three numbered examples: basic usage with return value comment, the dynamic shorthand method variant, and a practical pattern combining `hasChanged` with `changedFrom`.

function:changedProperties.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `changedProperties()` with three idiomatic examples: basic inspection of which properties changed, a conditional save guard, and an audit-log pattern combining `changedProperties()` with `changedFrom()`. The docblock and function signature were already accurate and needed no changes.

function:clearChangeInformation.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `clearChangeInformation` with three idiomatic examples: clearing a single property, clearing all properties, and a realistic `afterFind` callback pattern. The function source confirmed the behaviour — with no argument it resets all properties, with `property=` it resets only that one.

function:clearErrors.summary: Updated the clearErrors reference example to cover all four use cases: clearing all errors, clearing by property, clearing by name, and clearing by both property and name together. The docblock in errors.cfc already matched the documented hint and parameters exactly — no CFC changes needed.

function:compareTo.summary: Authored a new reference example for `compareTo` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/compareto.txt`. The two examples illustrate that the method compares object identity (not just data equality) and show a practical use-case of skipping the current user in a loop. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:delete.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `model.delete` to include four idiomatic examples: basic delete, permanent delete bypassing soft deletes, delete without callbacks, and the scoped association pattern. Model names updated to PascalCase per conventions.

function:errorCount.summary: Reviewed the `errorCount` function source (errors.cfc:113). Docblock, hint, and parameters all match the snapshot exactly. Rewrote the reference example to cover all three call variants: no arguments (total count), filtering by `property`, and filtering by `name`.

function:errorsOn.summary: Updated the `errorsOn` reference example to demonstrate three usage scenarios: basic property error retrieval, filtering by both property and error name, and iterating over the returned error array. The docblock in the CFC already matched the documented hint and parameters, so no CFC edits were needed.

function:errorsOnBase.summary: Expanded the `errorsOnBase` reference from a single line to three idiomatic examples covering: basic usage after validation, filtering by error name using the `name` parameter, and iterating over base errors. No CFC changes were needed — the docblock and implementation are consistent.

function:hasChanged.summary: Rewrote the `hasChanged` reference example with four clear, idiomatic scenarios: checking a specific property, checking any property (no argument), using the auto-generated dynamic shorthand (`statusHasChanged()`), and demonstrating that new unsaved objects always return `true`. The docblock and function signature were already accurate and required no changes.

function:hasErrors.summary: Updated the `hasErrors` reference example to cover three cases: basic error check, property-specific check, and name-specific check, matching the two documented optional parameters (`property` and `name`).

function:hasProperty.summary: Rewrote the `hasProperty` reference example with three clear, idiomatic examples: checking a set vs. unset property, the equivalent dynamic `has<PropertyName>()` method, and a guard-logic pattern before accessing a property. The docblock and function signature are already accurate and required no changes.

function:isNew.summary: Rewrote the `isNew` reference example with three idiomatic, well-commented examples covering: a newly instantiated (unsaved) object, a finder-loaded (persisted) object, and the primary use-case of branching logic inside a model callback. The original example had a syntax error (`model("employee").new()>`) and was minimal; the new version is clean and covers real-world usage.

function:isPersisted.summary: Created the reference example file for `isPersisted()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/ispersisted.txt` with three idiomatic examples covering: a new unsaved object (returns false), an object loaded via a finder (returns true), and a new object after saving (returns true). No docblock or source changes were needed.

function:key.summary: Wrote an improved reference example for `model.key` covering single primary key retrieval, dynamic programming usage, and composite keys. The existing one-liner was replaced with three numbered examples that illustrate the function's core value proposition as described in the docblock.

function:properties.summary: Expanded the reference example for `model.properties` from a single basic usage to three idiomatic examples covering: basic struct retrieval, excluding nested association properties with `returnIncluded=false`, and passing the struct to a service layer. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documentation are consistent.

function:propertyIsBlank.summary: Created the reference example file for `propertyIsBlank` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/propertyisblank.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating blank-check before notification, setting a default value, and branching logic contrasting with `propertyIsPresent`. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:propertyIsPresent.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `propertyIsPresent` to fix a syntax error in the original (malformed `">` on the empty-string line), remove non-idiomatic `return` statements, and add a third example demonstrating the false-when-property-missing case. Three clean numbered examples now cover: present value → true, empty string → false, missing property → false.

function:reload.summary: Wrote an updated reference example for `model.reload` with two idiomatic scenarios: reloading after an external DB-mutating call, and discarding in-memory property changes. The existing single example was preserved as example 1 (with minor capitalization fix to `Employee`) and a second example added to illustrate discarding unsaved changes.

function:setProperties.summary: Expanded the `setProperties` reference example to three idioms: updating from a form post struct, passing a struct literal, and using named arguments (as supported by the `$setProperties` internals). No docblock or code changes were needed — the existing source and documentation are consistent.

function:toggle.summary: Updated the toggle reference example to add a `save=false` usage (memory-only flip) and clarify the boolean return value. No CFC edits were made; the docblock hint about "Returns this object" is slightly misleading (code returns `true`, not `this`), but the discrepancy is minor and the example accurately reflects the actual boolean return behavior.

function:update.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `model.update` with 6 clean, numbered examples covering basic update, multi-property update with params, skipping validation, and the three association-scoped call patterns (hasOne setBio, hasMany addCar, hasMany removeComment). Fixed the existing file's formatting issue where `toggle` content was erroneously appended to the end of the `removeComment` line.

function:updateProperty.summary: Expanded the `updateProperty` reference example from one basic case to three idiomatic examples: a boolean flag use case (the documented primary purpose), marking a record active, and skipping callbacks. No docblock or source changes were needed.

function:valid.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `valid()` with three idiomatic examples covering basic validation, skipping callbacks, and validating associations. Fixed the original's missing parentheses around the `if` condition and improved code style throughout.

function:addFormat.summary: Verified the `addFormat` function in `vendor/wheels/Global.cfc` — the docblock, hint, and parameters all match the snapshot exactly. Updated the reference file with three numbered examples covering `js`, PowerPoint (`ppt`/`pptx`), and `csv` formats.

function:api.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/api.txt` with four examples demonstrating basic API scoping, combining `api()` with `version()` for versioned endpoints, overriding path/name prefixes, and using the callback form to avoid manual `.end()` calls. No docblock or body changes were needed — the source matched the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:collection.summary: Updated the `collection.txt` reference example with two numbered examples: one showing a GET search route on a photos collection, and one showing multiple collection routes (GET featured + POST bulkDelete) on articles. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint with no drift.

function:constraints.summary: Authored `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/constraints.txt` with two examples showing how `constraints()` restricts dynamic route segments via regex patterns (digits-only id, and multiple constraints for id + slug).

function:end.summary: The existing reference example for `mapper.end` was already correct and idiomatic. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint exactly, so no edits were needed. The reference file was confirmed and written as-is.

function:env.summary: Authored a new reference example for `env()` in `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/env.txt` with three idiomatic examples: reading a required variable, using a default fallback, and guarding startup on a missing secret. No docblock changes were needed — the source docblock matches the documented hint exactly.

function:get.summary: Updated the `mapper/get.txt` reference file to add numbered comment headings (per style guide) and a new example demonstrating the `redirect` parameter, which was documented but missing from the original examples. All existing usage patterns (slug routing, controller+action, custom patterns, package namespacing, nested resource member/collection routes) are preserved.

function:group.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/group.txt` with four idiomatic examples covering: basic path-prefix grouping, combined path+name prefix, regex constraints, and callback-style auto-closing groups. No docblock or code changes were needed; the source and documented hint are already in sync.

function:health.summary: Created vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/health.txt with three examples: default usage at /health, overriding the path and route name, and delegating to a custom controller action. No docblock or code changes were needed — the implementation matches the documented parameters exactly.

function:mapper.summary: Rewrote the mapper() reference example with three numbered examples: typical config/routes.cfm usage with namespace/resources/singular-resource/custom-named-routes/root, a `mapFormat=false` example, and a wildcard catch-all example. The docblock in Global.cfc already matched the documented parameters, so no CFC edits were needed.

function:member.summary: Updated the `member()` reference example to use numbered comment headings and added a second example demonstrating multiple member routes, matching the style of the adjacent `collection.txt` reference file. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:namespace.summary: Wrote the reference example for `mapper.namespace`, keeping the existing well-structured example and adding a clarifying comment explaining how `path` and `package` override the defaults derived from `name`. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint and parameters exactly (no edits needed).

function:package.summary: Updated the reference example for `mapper.package()` with three numbered examples: basic subfolder scoping without URL modification, overriding the subfolder name via the `package` argument, and nesting a `package` scope inside a resource to organize sub-resource controllers. The examples clearly demonstrate the key distinction from `namespace()` — no URL prefix is added.

function:patch.summary: The existing reference example was comprehensive and matched the docblock exactly. Updated the reference file to add a `redirect` parameter example (previously undocumented in the example) while preserving all existing examples. The docblock and function signature are already in sync — no CFC edits needed.

function:post.summary: Updated the `mapper/post.txt` reference example to add a `redirect` example (parameter #6) that was documented but previously undemonstrated, renumbered headings for clarity, and preserved all existing well-structured examples. The docblock in the CFC is consistent with the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits needed.

function:put.summary: Updated the `put` mapper reference example to add a `redirect` parameter demo (example 6) and renumbered all headings with descriptive comments, while preserving all existing examples. The docblock in the source CFC is already in sync with the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits were needed.

function:registerOnError.summary: Created the reference example file `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/registeronerror.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating: (1) basic error logging via `writeLog`, (2) multiple callbacks registered in order including one storing error state in application scope, and (3) a note on keeping callbacks fast with the isolation guarantee.

function:resource.summary: Updated the `resource` mapper reference example to fix an invalid `only="index"` action (singular resources don't support `index`; that's only for plural `resources()`), improved the `controller` path format to use `/` separator consistent with Wheels conventions, and added clearer numbered headings for each example scenario.
function:resource.notes: The original example used `only="index"` on a singular resource, but `index` is not a valid action for singular resources (valid actions are: show, new, create, edit, update, delete). Also changed `controller="sessions.auth"` to `controller="sessions/auth"` to match Wheels' subdirectory controller path convention.

function:resources.summary: Updated the `resources` reference example to expand coverage from 6 examples to 9, adding demonstrations of `shallow` nesting, `constraints`, and `singular` override — all parameters documented but previously absent from the reference. The existing examples were preserved and renumbered with consistent numbered comment headings.

function:root.summary: Rewrote the `root` reference example with three numbered examples covering: (1) basic web root mapping with `to`, (2) namespace-scoped root using separate `controller`/`action` arguments, and (3) namespace root with `mapFormat=true` to capture format extensions. The docblock already correctly documents all parameters including `controller` and `action`.

function:scope.summary: Updated the reference example for `mapper.scope` to add two new examples demonstrating the `name` prefix and `constraints` parameters, while preserving and reformatting the three existing examples with numbered comment headings. The docblock in the CFC is accurate and needed no changes.

function:set.summary: Rewrote the reference file for `set()` with numbered comment headings, fixing the trailing `:` typo on the old example 3, and adding a 4th example showing how to apply defaults to multiple helper functions at once using a comma-delimited `functionName` list (supported by `$set()` internals).

function:switchTenant.summary: Created the reference example file for `switchTenant` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/switchtenant.txt` with three idiomatic examples: basic data-source-only switch, full tenant struct with id and config, and force-switching past a TenantResolver lock.

function:tenant.summary: Created the reference example file for `tenant()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/tenant.txt` with three idiomatic examples: basic retrieval, checking if a tenant is active, and accessing per-tenant config values. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented hint were consistent.

function:version.summary: Created the reference example file for `mapper.version` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/version.txt`. The file demonstrates three examples: basic versioned API routing with `api()` + `version()`, using a callback for nested route definitions, and overriding the default path/name prefixes.

function:whereAlpha.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/wherealpha.txt` with three examples demonstrating: (1) constraining a single route variable to alphabetic characters, (2) constraining multiple variables via a comma-delimited list, and (3) chaining with `whereNumber` for mixed-type constraints. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documentation are in sync.

function:whereAlphaNumeric.summary: Created the reference example for `whereAlphaNumeric()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/wherealphanumeric.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: constraining a single variable, constraining multiple comma-delimited variables at once, and chaining after a `resources()` block. No docblock changes were needed — the existing hint and `@variableName` param are accurate.

function:whereIn.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/wherein.txt` with three idiomatic examples showing how to constrain route variables to a fixed set of allowed values using `whereIn()`, including chaining with other constraint helpers like `whereNumber()`.

function:whereMatch.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/wherematch.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating `whereMatch()`: constraining a year placeholder to a 4-digit pattern, a slug to lowercase-alphanumeric-hyphen, and a product key to a SKU format after a `resources()` call. No docblock changes were needed — the source docblock and snapshot parameters are in sync.

function:whereNumber.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/wherenumber.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating single-variable constraint, comma-delimited multi-variable constraint, and chaining with `whereAlpha()`. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:whereSlug.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/whereslug.txt` with three examples demonstrating: constraining a single slug variable, chaining after a resources route, and constraining multiple slug variables via a comma-delimited list. The docblock in the source was already consistent with the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits were needed.

function:whereUuid.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/mapper/whereuuid.txt` with three idiomatic examples showing single-variable UUID constraint, comma-delimited multi-variable constraint, and chaining with other `where*` helpers. The docblock and parameters were already accurate; no CFC edits needed.

function:wildcard.summary: Rewrote the `wildcard` reference example with five numbered scenarios covering the basic case, `mapKey`, `mapFormat`, a custom `action` default, and multiple HTTP methods. The existing example used `methods=` (the alias) for the last case; updated to use the canonical `method=` parameter name while keeping the same intent. No CFC changes were needed.

function:accessibleProperties.summary: Updated the `accessibleProperties` reference example to include two clear usage examples: one for a single property and one for a comma-delimited list of properties, both placed inside `config()` as required by the function's context.

function:afterCreate.summary: Enhanced the `afterCreate` reference example with three idiomatic examples: registering a single callback method shown in context of a model `config()` block, registering multiple methods via a comma-delimited list, and using the named `methods` argument form. No code or docblock changes were needed.

function:afterDelete.summary: Expanded the `afterDelete` reference example from a single one-liner to three idiomatic examples demonstrating: (1) registering a single callback method, (2) passing a comma-separated list of methods in one call, and (3) chaining multiple individual `afterDelete` calls. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:afterFind.summary: Updated the afterFind reference example with three numbered examples: basic single-callback registration, a realistic field-formatting use case, and registering multiple callbacks as a comma-delimited list. The examples demonstrate the key behavior that the callback receives row columns as `arguments` and can modify them by returning the struct.

function:afterInitialization.summary: Updated the `afterInitialization` reference example to use numbered headings and added a second example demonstrating a comma-delimited list of callback methods. The docblock and function signature are consistent with the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits needed.

function:afterNew.summary: Updated the `afterNew` reference example with three idiomatic examples: a full model config() context showing a single callback with its implementation, a comma-delimited multi-method list, and the `method` argument alias. The docblock and signature are accurate and required no changes.

function:afterSave.summary: Expanded the `afterSave` reference example from a single bare call to three idiomatic examples showing single-method registration, comma-delimited multi-method list, and multiple chained `afterSave()` calls — all placed inside a model's `config()` method as is conventional. No docblock or source changes were needed.

function:afterUpdate.summary: Rewrote the afterUpdate reference example with three idiomatic examples matching the style of afterCreate: single method registration, comma-delimited list, and named `methods` argument. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:afterValidation.summary: Expanded the `afterValidation` reference example to three idiomatic cases: single method, comma-delimited list of methods, and use of the `method` singular alias. No docblock or code changes were needed — source and docs are already in sync.

function:afterValidationOnCreate.summary: Expanded the reference example for `afterValidationOnCreate` from a single line to three idiomatic examples showing: a single method with a practical body, a comma-delimited list of methods, and the `method` alias — all placed inside a `config()` block within a model component, matching the style of sibling callback reference files.

function:afterValidationOnUpdate.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `afterValidationOnUpdate` with two idiomatic examples: one showing a single callback method in a model's `config()`, and one demonstrating a comma-separated list of multiple methods. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented hint are consistent.

function:automaticValidations.summary: Updated the reference example for `automaticValidations` to include two idiomatic examples: one disabling and one enabling automatic validations, both shown in a proper model `config()` context with `component extends="Model"` wrappers.

function:beforeCreate.summary: Expanded the beforeCreate reference example from a single minimal line to three numbered examples demonstrating single callback registration, multiple callbacks as a comma-separated list, and use of the named `methods` argument.

function:beforeDelete.summary: Expanded the `beforeDelete` reference from a single one-liner to three idiomatic examples: registering a single cleanup callback, registering multiple callbacks via a comma-delimited list, and halting deletion by returning false from the callback. No CFC edits were needed — the docblock and implementation are consistent.

function:beforeSave.summary: Expanded the `beforeSave` reference example from a single one-liner to three idiomatic examples demonstrating a single method callback, a comma-delimited list of methods, and the `method` argument alias — all consistent with the CFC source and Wheels conventions.

function:beforeUpdate.summary: Expanded the `beforeUpdate` reference example from a single line to three idiomatic examples matching the style of `beforesave.txt`: single method, comma-delimited list, and `method` argument alias. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:beforeValidation.summary: Expanded the `beforeValidation` reference example from a single one-liner into three idiomatic examples demonstrating: (1) registering a single method to normalize data before validation, (2) passing a comma-delimited list of multiple methods, and (3) stacking multiple `beforeValidation` calls in `config()`. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented hint are consistent.

function:beforeValidationOnCreate.summary: Expanded the `beforeValidationOnCreate` reference example from a single minimal line to three idiomatic examples covering single-method, multi-method (comma-separated list), and the `method` argument alias — consistent with the documented parameter hint and sibling callback functions.

function:beforeValidationOnUpdate.summary: Expanded the `beforeValidationOnUpdate` reference example from a single minimal line to three examples demonstrating a single callback, a comma-delimited list of callbacks, and the `method` argument alias. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matched the documented hint and parameters.

function:belongsTo.summary: Updated the `belongsTo` reference file with four idiomatic examples covering basic usage, custom naming, `joinType="outer"`, and the `polymorphic=true` option. Also added the missing `@polymorphic` param hint to the CFC docblock to match the documented parameter table.

function:dataSource.summary: Updated the `dataSource` reference example to include a basic usage example (matching the original) and a second example demonstrating the optional `username` and `password` parameters. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documentation are in sync.

function:enum.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/enum.txt` with three idiomatic examples showing: (1) basic list-based enum for a `status` property, (2) struct-based enum mapping names to numeric stored values, and (3) runtime usage of the generated `is<Value>()` checker methods and named query scopes.

function:hasMany.summary: Updated the `hasMany` docblock to add the missing `@as` parameter documentation, and rewrote the reference example file with 8 idiomatic examples covering basic usage, many-to-many shortcuts, all four `dependent` modes, custom foreign keys, join type override, and polymorphic associations.

function:hasOne.summary: Fixed the reference file for `hasOne`: the third example incorrectly called `hasMany` instead of `hasOne` — corrected and reformatted with numbered headings. Added a fourth example showing the polymorphic `as` usage. Also added the missing `@as` docblock annotation to the `hasOne` function in associations.cfc.

function:ignoredColumns.summary: Rewrote the `ignoredColumns` reference example with two clear, idiomatic examples: one showing a single column being ignored, and another showing multiple columns. Both demonstrate proper usage inside a model's `config()` method using the named `columns` array argument.

function:nestedProperties.summary: Rewrote the `nestedProperties` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering the main parameters: `allowDelete`, `autoSave`+`rejectIfBlank`, `sortProperty`, and multiple associations via the `associations` alias. No docblock drift was found; the CFC source matched the documented parameters exactly.

function:property.summary: Updated the `property()` reference example to retain all four original examples while adding two new ones covering the `dataType`/`select` combination and `automaticValidations` parameters, ensuring all documented parameters are illustrated.

function:protectedProperties.summary: Validated the `protectedProperties` function source — docblock and signature match the documented hint and parameters exactly. Updated the reference example to include two idiomatic examples: one with a comma-delimited list (positional) and one using the named `properties` argument form.

function:setPrimaryKey.summary: Expanded the `setPrimaryKey` reference example from a single minimal snippet to two examples: one showing a custom single primary key, and one demonstrating composite primary keys using the `setPrimaryKeys()` alias. The docblock in the CFC was already accurate and needed no changes.

function:setPrimaryKeys.summary: Wrote two idiomatic examples for `setPrimaryKeys()` — a two-column composite key (Subscription model) and a three-column composite key (OrderItem model). The docblock and source were already consistent with the documented hint and parameters; no CFC edits were needed.

function:setTableNamePrefix.summary: Verified that the docblock in miscellaneous.cfc exactly matches the documented hint and parameters. Updated the reference example with a cleaner first example and a second example showing a schema-style prefix, covering the most common real-world use cases.

function:sharedModel.summary: Created reference example for `sharedModel()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/sharedmodel.txt`. The examples demonstrate calling `sharedModel()` inside a model's `config()` method for both a `Tenant` model and a `Plan` lookup table, illustrating the multi-tenancy use case where these models always use the central application datasource.

function:table.summary: Wrote an expanded reference example for `model/table.txt` with three examples: mapping to a custom table name, mapping to a legacy-prefixed table, and calling `table(false)` to declare a model with no backing table. No docblock changes were needed — the CFC source exactly matches the documented hint and parameters.

function:validate.summary: Expanded the `validate` reference example from one basic usage to five numbered examples covering: basic registration, multiple methods via the `methods` argument, limiting to `onUpdate`/`onCreate` via `when`, conditional execution via `condition`, and conditional skipping via `unless`. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and snapshot are in sync.

function:validateOnCreate.summary: Updated the reference example for `validateOnCreate` with three idiomatic examples: basic single-method registration, registering a comma-delimited list of methods, and using the `condition` argument to conditionally run validation on create.

function:validateOnUpdate.summary: Updated the `validateOnUpdate` reference example with three idiomatic examples: basic single-method registration, multiple methods via comma-delimited list, and conditional validation using the `condition` argument. The docblock in the CFC is accurate and no changes were needed there.

function:validatesConfirmationOf.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validatesConfirmationOf` with three numbered examples covering: basic password confirmation on create, email confirmation with the default `onSave` timing, and multiple properties with case-sensitive comparison. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:validatesExclusionOf.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validatesExclusionOf` with four idiomatic examples covering: basic single-property exclusion with custom message, multiple properties, `when`/`allowBlank` combination, and conditional validation via `condition`. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented parameters.

function:validatesFormatOf.summary: Rewrote the `validatesFormatOf` reference example with proper formatting and 4 numbered examples covering: type-based credit card validation, email type validation, regex with allowBlank and custom message, and the conditional Swedish email check from the original. The existing file was a single unreadable line; the new file is properly formatted and idiomatic.

function:validatesInclusionOf.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validatesInclusionOf` with four numbered examples covering basic usage, `allowBlank`, `when` (onCreate-only), and `condition`-based validation. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented parameters — no source edits were needed.

function:validatesLengthOf.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validatesLengthOf` with three numbered examples covering `maximum` with multiple properties, `within` range validation (fixing the comment/code mismatch where the comment said "4 and 15" but the code used `within="4,20"`), and `exactly` with `when` and `allowBlank` arguments.

function:validatesNumericalityOf.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validatesNumericalityOf` with 6 idiomatic examples covering basic numeric validation, integer-only with allowBlank, range constraints (greaterThan/lessThanOrEqualTo), create-only integer ranges, conditional equality, and odd-number enforcement. Also fixed the existing example's typo ("Tetting" → properly rewritten). The docblock has a minor formatting issue (`@oddSpecifies`/`@evenSpecifies` missing a space) but the parameters are documented in the snapshot table, so no CFC edit was needed.

function:validatesPresenceOf.summary: Expanded the `validatesPresenceOf` reference from a single minimal example to 6 numbered examples covering basic usage, multiple properties, custom message, `when` limiting validation to create/update, and conditional validation via `condition` and `unless`. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters are consistent.

function:validatesUniquenessOf.summary: Rewrote the reference file for `validatesUniquenessOf` with 7 idiomatic examples covering the core use case, scoped uniqueness, multi-property lists, allowBlank, when, condition, and includeSoftDeletes — all consistent with the documented parameters and source implementation.
